framework.name: OAuth 2.0 Scope-Based Authorization Model
framework.description: OAuth 2.0 defines authorization through 'scopes' — granular permissions that clients request and resource servers enforce. Scopes are text strings (e.g., 'read:email', 'write:posts') that define what actions are permitted. This is enforced via access tokens that carry scope metadata.
framework.raw_quote: OAuth 2.0 is the industry-standard protocol for authorization. OAuth 2.0 focuses on client developer simplicity while providing specific authorization flows for web applications, desktop applications, mobile phones, and living room devices.
